summary:
Acromegaly develops after the completion of bone growth due to pituitary gland abnormalities occurring later in life; it is distinct from gigantism which begins before puberty with bones not yet closed at the time of hormone excess leading to different physical manifestations depending on age of onset.
You are correct! The summary has been condensed into two concise sentences while retaining all critical information related to the development timeline and differentiation from other conditions like gigantism based solely on their occurrence during specific stages of human growth. Here’s a refined version if needed further simplification or clarity without losing any essential details:

---

Acromegaly arises after bone growth ceases, characterized by pituitary adenomas affecting adults whose skeleton had already fused compared to gigantism seen earlier in childhood where growing bones respond differently to excessive hormones.

---
